preNhl.note: The pre-NHL production record. Scoring is NOT comparable across leagues or to the NHL -- this is the biography, not a translation.

tradeComps.note: Real trades of players closest to him by PRODUCTION (points rank among same-season peers), age, contract AND years of control — so a controlled scorer matches other controlled scorers, not rentals. The return is only what his old team got back FROM the team that landed him; “package” means he wasn't the only piece going out, so that return isn't his alone. For the genuinely elite — who almost never trade, so their only comps are distressed sales — we show a MODELED estimate of what a player his caliber would command instead.

value.summary: His total on-ice value, converted to WINS (WAR). It adds up everything he does — even-strength play, the power play and penalty kill, his penalty discipline RELATIVE TO HIS POSITION (defensemen structurally take more than they draw, so only the differential vs other D counts), and faceoffs — and the grade ranks that total against other players at his position over the last three seasons.
value.method: Total WAR sums these four, each priced in wins. The grade is this player's percentile vs same-position peers over the 3-season window.

hits60.method: Per-60 rate over 3 seasons (TOI-weighted), ranked vs same-position peers.
hits60.label: Physicality
hits60.summary: Physicality is how often he throws a body check — counted straight from the NHL's play-by-play. He's logged 48 hits over his last 27 games (7.8 per 60 minutes on the ice), and the grade ranks that against other defensemen.
hits60.caveat: Home-scorer count -- arena bias is real (some rinks record far more than others). Read big gaps as signal, small ones as noise.

blocks60.method: Per-60 rate over 3 seasons (TOI-weighted), ranked vs same-position peers.
blocks60.label: Shot blocking
blocks60.summary: Shot blocking is how willing he is to get in a shooting lane — counted straight from the NHL's play-by-play. He's logged 26 blocked shots over his last 27 games (4.2 per 60 minutes on the ice), and the grade ranks that against other defensemen.
blocks60.caveat: Home-scorer count -- arena bias is real (some rinks record far more than others). Read big gaps as signal, small ones as noise.

takeaways60.method: Per-60 rate over 3 seasons (TOI-weighted), ranked vs same-position peers.
takeaways60.label: Puck stripping
takeaways60.summary: Puck stripping is how often he strips the puck off an opponent — counted straight from the NHL's play-by-play. He's logged 0 takeaways over his last 27 games (0.0 per 60 minutes on the ice), and the grade ranks that against other defensemen.
takeaways60.caveat: Home-scorer count -- arena bias is real (some rinks record far more than others). Read big gaps as signal, small ones as noise.

giveaways60.method: Per-60 rate over 3 seasons (TOI-weighted), ranked vs same-position peers.
giveaways60.label: Puck security
giveaways60.summary: Puck security is how often he turns the puck over (fewer is better) — counted straight from the NHL's play-by-play. He's logged 7 giveaways over his last 27 games (1.1 per 60 minutes on the ice), and the grade ranks that against other defensemen.
giveaways60.caveat: Home-scorer count -- arena bias is real (some rinks record far more than others). Read big gaps as signal, small ones as noise.

primary_assists60.method: Per-60 rate over 3 seasons (TOI-weighted), ranked vs same-position peers.
primary_assists60.label: Playmaking
primary_assists60.summary: Playmaking is how often he makes the primary pass on a goal — 0.32 per 60 minutes of ice time over his last 3 seasons, ranked against other defensemen.

rush_xg60.method: Per-60 rate over 3 seasons (TOI-weighted), ranked vs same-position peers.
rush_xg60.label: Rush offense
rush_xg60.summary: Rush offense is the danger of the chances he creates off the rush — 0.02 per 60 minutes of ice time over his last 3 seasons, ranked against other defensemen.

skills.window: recent 3 seasons, TOI-weighted, vs position peers
skills.note: hits/takeaways/giveaways are RTSS-tracked (arena scorer bias, not adjusted); giveaways shown as puck security (100 = fewest). All-situations TOI denominates rates.

context.note: From shift overlaps. Headline is usage (ice time of the players around him); production is the model's grade of those players. A big gap means leaned-on help that grades below its minutes.

meta.war: Total WAR = EV (5v5 RAPM) + special teams + penalties + faceoffs + finishing. xG-based play-driving plus shrunk shooting talent; single-season RAPM is noisy -- the pooled multi-year RAPM is the stable read.
meta.rapm: off/def/total RAPM are net xG per 60 at 5v5, teammate- and competition-adjusted (ridge regression over shift stints).
meta.coverage: On-ice metrics cover seasons with shift data: 2010-11 to present (100% of regular-season games).

_explain.summary: Petter Granberg's -0.04 total WAR in 2015-16 ranks in the 22nd percentile of NHL skaters. It breaks down as: even-strength play (5v5): -0.04 WAR (15th percentile); special teams (PP/PK): +0.00 WAR (37th percentile); finishing (shooting talent): +0.00 WAR (58th percentile); penalty discipline (vs position): -0.00 WAR (50th percentile); faceoffs: +0.00 WAR (31st percentile).
_explain.narrative: Petter Granberg's -0.04 WAR in 2015-16 is driven most by special teams (PP/PK) (+0.00, 37th percentile) -- within 5v5 he tilts toward defense (offense -0.20/60, 3rd percentile; defense +0.01/60, 52nd percentile). His weakest area is even-strength play (5v5) (-0.04, 15th percentile). (xG-based; single-season RAPM is noisy -- see multi-year RAPM for the stable read.)
